rows.description: Computus Dni Rectoris. Johannis Collier Jones.
rows.notes: Clear centered page title in Latin handwriting. No amounts visible on this line; letters are legible.
Date line directly under title; handwriting clear and contains no monetary figures.
Continuation of heading indicating the period to 1820; no amounts present and text is readable.
Section heading (appears to read 'Arrearagia recepta' though spelling is archaic/irregular); no amounts on this line.
Clear section heading introducing fixed money rents. No monetary figures on this line.
Amount column alignment is clear (20 0 0). The description is faint/cursive and uncertain (read as 'Mushennicot'); chosen reading matches letter shapes but not certain.
Description reads 'Rose. St Martin's' (abbreviation 'Rose.' visible). Amount 1 10 0 is clearly aligned in the £/s/d columns.

Amount 2 15 0 is clear. The name portion is cursive and read as 'Captn Sloman for Kidlington' (Captain abbreviation 'Captn' inferred from the short form visible); alternative readings possible for the personal name.
Amount 9 6 0 clear. Description partly abbreviated ('said Lane') then a surname read as 'Norris'; handwriting of the surname is legible but the preceding word is abbreviated.
Amount 6 13 4 is well aligned and within valid ranges. Description read as 'Dunnithorne' from cursive; letters are somewhat flourished so minor uncertainty remains.
Amount 1 13 4 clear and aligned. Surname 'Pickford' is legible in the ledger script.
Section heading clearly reads 'Land Tax redeemed.' and separates the previous group from the following amounts.
Amount 22 5 6 aligns with the pounds/shillings/pence columns. 'Kidlington Rectory' is clearly written and unambiguous.
'Do Vicarage' (ditto for Vicarage) is clear; amount 4 19 0 is well-placed and valid.
Description 'Long Whittenham Rectory' is legible. Amount 28 0 0 is clear and aligned with column lines.
Repeated ditto 'Do Vicarage' with amount 4 4 0 clearly visible and in-range.
Section heading introducing variable mixed rents; clearly written and contains no monetary figures itself.
Amount 134 5 9 is clearly aligned. Description reads 'L. Whittenham Rectory to Mich. 1819' — the leading initial 'L.' and date are legible though the abbreviation spacing is slightly cramped.
Amount 172 14 0 clearly placed. Description 'Kidlington Augmentation. Do' read from cursive; 'Do' likely indicates ditto/continued note — reading of 'Augmentation' is clear.
Amount 232 1 9 aligns well. Description 'S. Newington Rectory' is legible; initial 'S.' likely denotes 'St' or similar.
Amount 64 12 11 is clear. Description read as 'Yarnton Rectory. to Mich. 1810' — the year's final digit is slightly cramped (read as '1810'), minor uncertainty in the date digits.

Amount reads 22 pounds, 1 shilling, 8½ pence — the halfpenny symbol (loop/'ob') after the 8 is visible and interpreted as 0.5. Column alignment is consistent. Description 'Kidlington Mills. to Mich. 1819' is legible.
Amount 11 0 6 clear. Description read as 'Clifton Ferry. Do' (Do = ditto/continued); handwriting clear for this short entry.
Amount 3 3 0 is clearly aligned. Description 'Grant's Fish Shop. to Mich. 1819' is readable; apostrophe and words are distinct.

Summation line beneath mixed rents; amount reads as 741 pounds, 14 shillings, 5¼ pence (quarter-farthings shown as '1/4' after the pence). The total is well-aligned under the columns; description area is blank on the page.
New section heading clearly reads 'Recepta Anni presentis.' (receipts of the present year). No amounts on this heading line.
Subheading 'Redeemed Land Tax.' clearly separates the next small group of receipts; handwriting readable.
Amount 4 19 0 clear in the £/s/d columns. Description reads as 'Kidlington Vicarage - to Mich 1820'; 'Mich' abbreviation and year are legible.
Amount 4 4 0 is clear. Description uses ditto abbreviations 'Do - do' after 'Long Whittenham' — handwriting is slightly cramped but readable.
Subheading repeating mixed rents for the present year; clearly written, no monetary figures on this line.

rows.description: S. Newington Rectory - part to Feb. 28. 1820
rows.notes: Amount 103 12 6 is aligned and legible. The description contains a contraction/abbreviation and a date 'to Feb. 28. 1820' — handwriting of the portion 'part' / abbreviation is moderately unclear, so 'part' is supplied as best interpretation.

rows.description: Recd. for Grant's Fish Shop 1 1/2 to Mich. 1820
rows.notes: Amount 3 3 0 clear. Description reads 'Recd. for Grant's Fish Shop 1 1/2 to Mich. 1820' — the '1 1/2' (one and a half years/portion) is visible in the text; minor uncertainty in exact punctuation but words are readable.
Final subtotal for the 'Recepta Anni presentis' column reads 118 10 6 and is well-aligned under the £/s/d columns. No textual description is present on the line itself.
